# Recommended Approach — Flux Image-to-Image (Self-Hosted Qwen-Image-Edit, Apache 2.0)

**Generated:** 2026-04-23
**Generated By:** @architect (Aria)
**Task:** `analyze-project-structure.md` (WIS-15, Phase 1)
**Companion docs:** [`project-analysis.md`](./project-analysis.md) · [`cost-comparison-i2i-providers.md`](./cost-comparison-i2i-providers.md) · [`research-commercial-i2i-models.md`](./research-commercial-i2i-models.md)
**Formalized in:** [`adr-0003-image-to-image-model-selection.md`](./adr-0003-image-to-image-model-selection.md)

**Supersedes prior drafts:** this document went through three iterations during 2026-04-23 as constraints were clarified:

| Version | Decision | Blocker that moved us forward |
|---|---|---|
| v0.1 | Self-host FLUX.1-Kontext-[dev] | FLUX Non-Commercial License — blocked portfolio-wide commercial use |
| v0.2 | BFL FLUX.2 [pro] hosted API ($0.045/img) | Perpetual per-image vendor cost at portfolio scale; vendor lock; more expensive than Gemini for alpha volumes |
| **v0.3 (current)** | **Self-host Qwen-Image-Edit (Apache 2.0)** | Accepted |

---

## 1. Feature Requirements

**Description:** Extend servegate to accept `(input image + text instruction) → edited image`. Capability must be reusable as portfolio infrastructure across future commercial apps.

**Constraints captured from user (2026-04-23):**

1. Commercial license required **at the weight level** — not a vendor SaaS TOS — to permit perpetual reuse across the product portfolio without per-seat / per-MAU caps or vendor approval gates.
2. Per-image cost must not scale linearly with a vendor invoice across portfolio apps.
3. Existing RunPod Serverless stack must be reusable; do not stand up new infrastructure.

These three constraints together eliminate every hosted API (BFL, Gemini, OpenAI) and every Non-Commercial-weight model (FLUX-[dev], Kontext-[dev], FLUX.2-[dev], Fill, Redux, SD3 Large NC variants).

---

## 2. Feasibility Verdict

**Viable. Self-hosting Qwen-Image-Edit on the existing RunPod endpoint is the correct decision.**

- The model is **Apache 2.0** — unconditional commercial redistribution, fine-tuning, bundling into derivative products, all permitted without notice to or approval from the publisher.
- **fp8 quantization fits the RTX 4090** (~20.4 GB VRAM, ~86% utilization per ComfyUI benchmarks).
- **Lightning 8-step LoRA** (community-distributed under Apache-compatible terms) reduces inference to ~34 seconds/image warm at 1024².
- Estimated unit cost **~$0.009/image** on RunPod Serverless Flex — roughly 5× cheaper than BFL FLUX.2 [pro] and 4× cheaper than Gemini 2.5 Flash Image Standard tier.
- Existing async submit/poll contract (ADR-0002) absorbs i2i with zero contract change.
- Existing `serverless/handler.py` absorbs i2i as a new workflow branch — no new infrastructure required.

Quality is **~80-85% of Flux Kontext** on worst-case localized precision edits (documented per community benchmarks and head-to-head reports in `research-commercial-i2i-models.md` §6). Known gotchas are engineering-tractable. See §5 for mitigation strategy.

---

## 3. Model Selection — Qwen-Image-Edit

**Decision:** `Qwen/Qwen-Image-Edit` (Alibaba, released 2025-08-19, Apache 2.0).

Specific artifacts to deploy on the RunPod network volume:

| Artifact | Size | License |
|---|---|---|
| `qwen_image_edit_fp8_e4m3fn.safetensors` (UNet) | ~20.4 GB | Apache 2.0 |
| `qwen_image_vae.safetensors` | ~160 MB | Apache 2.0 |
| `qwen_2.5_vl_7b_fp8_scaled.safetensors` (text/image encoder) | ~8 GB | Apache 2.0 |
| Lightning 8-step LoRA | ~150 MB | Apache-compatible community release |
| **Total additional storage** | **~29 GB** | — |

At $0.07/GB/month network volume pricing: **~$2/month additional fixed storage cost.**

### Alternatives rejected (summary)

| Model | License | Rejection reason |
|---|---|---|
| OmniGen2 | Apache 2.0 | Community: *"not quite there yet"* — quality insufficient |
| HiDream-E1.1 | MIT transformer + **Llama 3.1 Community License** encoder | Not pure Apache — Llama attribution + MAU cap |
| FLUX.1-schnell + ControlNet + IP-Adapter | Apache 2.0 | Cannot do instruction editing; wrong tool |
| FLUX.2 [dev], FLUX.1 Kontext-[dev], Fill, Redux | FLUX Non-Commercial | License forbids commercial use |
| SD 3.5 Medium/Large | Stability Community ($1M cap) | Not pure commercial; no native i2i |
| SDXL img2img | CreativeML OpenRAIL-M | Commercial OK but with use restrictions; older model, weaker quality |
| BFL hosted API (FLUX.2 [pro]) | SaaS TOS | Perpetual per-image invoice; vendor lock |
| Gemini 2.5 Flash Image | Google API TOS | Perpetual per-token invoice; vendor lock |
| Z-Image-Edit | Apache 2.0 (announced) | **Not released** as of April 2026; watch-list |
| Step1X-Edit | Apache 2.0 (to verify) | Community reception not yet mature; watch-list |

Full candidate analysis in [`research-commercial-i2i-models.md`](./research-commercial-i2i-models.md).

---

## 4. Architecture — What Changes, What Doesn't

### 4.1 Data flow

```
Client (SDK v0.3.0 with .edit())
   └── POST /jobs  { prompt, input_image_b64, strength?, ... }
           │
           ▼
Cloudflare Worker Gateway  (unchanged — opaque passthrough per CON-3)
   ├── validateAuth (X-API-Key)
   ├── checkAndIncrement rate-limit (100/day global)
   ├── submitJob → RunPod /run  (same endpoint as T2I today)
   ├── crypto.randomUUID() → job_id
   ├── putMapping(JOBS_KV, {job_id ⇄ runpod_request_id})
   └── 202 Accepted { job_id, status_url, est_wait_seconds:"unknown" }

   ▼ (SDK polls every 5s, Retry-After header — unchanged from ADR-0002)
GET /jobs/{id}
   ├── validateAuth
   ├── getMapping(JOBS_KV, job_id)
   ├── getStatus → RunPod /status/{runpod_request_id}
   └── mapStatus → queued|running (202) | completed (200 + output) | failed (500) | timeout (504)

   ▼
RunPod Serverless worker  (serverless/handler.py)
   ├── wait_for_comfy
   ├── normalize_input → validates required+optional fields (see §4.3)
   ├── BRANCH by payload:
   │     ┌── input_image_b64 present → build_qwen_edit_workflow()
   │     │                                ├── load Qwen UNet fp8
   │     │                                ├── load Lightning 8-step LoRA
   │     │                                ├── load Qwen2.5-VL encoder
   │     │                                ├── LoadImageBase64 → VAEEncode → latent
   │     │                                ├── CLIPTextEncode (positive+negative)
   │     │                                ├── KSampler (denoise=strength, 8 steps)
   │     │                                ├── VAEDecode → pixel
   │     │                                ├── post-process: match output dims to input (mitigation §5)
   │     │                                └── SaveImage
   │     └── else (T2I, current)         → build_t2i_workflow()  [unchanged]
   ├── queue_workflow → ComfyUI /prompt
   ├── poll_history (280s timeout)
   ├── extract_image_bytes
   └── return { image_b64, metadata: { seed, elapsed_ms } }
```

### 4.2 What doesn't change

| Component | Reason |
|---|---|
| `gateway/src/index.ts` — routing, auth, rate-limit, KV mapping | Gateway is opaque passthrough (CON-3); zero i2i awareness needed |
| `gateway/src/runpod.ts` | Same RunPod endpoint for both T2I and i2i |
| Async submit/poll contract | ADR-0002 unchanged |
| `AD-1` (image_b64 inline in output) | Preserved — Qwen output is base64-encoded inline by the handler, same as schnell today |
| `CON-3` (handler-as-black-box to gateway) | Preserved |
| `CON-4` (never log prompt or image bytes) | Preserved — extended to log neither input nor output image bytes for i2i |
| Auth, X-API-Key, rate-limit (100/day global) | Unchanged |
| Existing T2I path (schnell 4-step, fp8, ~$0.001/img) | **Unchanged byte-for-byte** |
| Network volume region (`mqqgzwnfp1`, US-IL-1) | Reused; additive upload only |
| RunPod datacenter constraint (US-IL-1) | Unchanged |
| ADR-0001 cold-start decision (Path A) | Unchanged; see §5 for i2i-specific cold-start note |

### 4.3 What changes

#### `serverless/handler.py`

**`normalize_input` extension:**

```python
input_image_b64: Optional[str]  # base64 PNG/JPEG; presence triggers i2i branch
strength: float = 0.85          # 0.0 < strength ≤ 1.0; maps to KSampler denoise
# steps default changes based on branch:
#   T2I: default 4 (schnell)
#   i2i: default 8 (Qwen + Lightning LoRA)
```

Validation (new for i2i branch):

- Reject exact 1:1 aspect ratio (converts to 16:15 or 15:16 automatically OR errors — product decision during implementation).
- Enforce ≤1 MP input (downsample if client sent larger — defensive; SDK should also catch this).
- Reject unsupported MIME types.
- Base64 decode + PIL-based image validation (dimensions, channels).

**`build_qwen_edit_workflow(prompt, input_image_b64, strength, seed, steps)`** — new ComfyUI graph composer. Reference sketch in [`adr-0003-image-to-image-model-selection.md`](./adr-0003-image-to-image-model-selection.md) §"Implementation Notes".

**`handler()` dispatch:**

```python
params = normalize_input(job.get("input") or {})
if params.get("input_image_b64"):
    workflow = build_qwen_edit_workflow(**params)
else:
    workflow = build_t2i_workflow(**params)  # unchanged
```

**Post-processing step (new):** after ComfyUI returns the generated PNG, resize to match input image dimensions before base64 encoding. Mitigates the known "aspect ratio drift" gotcha (§5).

#### `sdk/` — v0.3.0 (minor, additive)

**`sdk/src/types.ts`:**

```typescript
export interface EditInput {
  prompt: string;
  image: Buffer | Uint8Array | Blob | string;  // binary or base64 string
  strength?: number;                             // 0.0-1.0, default 0.85
  seed?: number;
  steps?: number;                                // default 8 (Lightning)
  aspect_ratio?: string;                         // e.g., "16:9"; SDK rejects "1:1"
}

// EditOutput reuses GenerateOutput shape (image_b64 inline per AD-1)
```

**`sdk/src/client.ts`:**

```typescript
async edit(input: EditInput): Promise<GenerateOutput> {
  // 1. Normalize image to base64 (from Buffer/Blob/string)
  // 2. Validate ≤1 MP; downsample via sharp/canvas if larger
  // 3. Reject exact 1:1 aspect ratio with clear ValidationError
  // 4. POST /jobs with { prompt, input_image_b64, strength, ... }
  // 5. Reuse existing submit/poll machinery (same path as generate())
}
```

**`sdk/CHANGELOG.md`** — v0.3.0 entry.

#### Documentation

- `docs/api/reference.md` — new `/jobs` request variant with `input_image_b64`, `strength`, constraint list (1 MP, 1:1 rejection).
- `sdk/README.md` + `sdk/README.pt-BR.md` — new `edit()` section.
- `docs/usage/dev-onboarding.md` — optional second quickstart section (keep T2I intro path clean).
- **`docs/architecture/adr-0003-image-to-image-model-selection.md`** — already created; formalizes the decision.
- `docs/legal/TERMS.md` + `TERMS.pt-BR.md` — **no third-party data-processor clause required** (weights run on our infra). Minor update: declare that user-submitted images are processed on our compute only, not transmitted to external vendors.
- `docs/legal/PRIVACY.md` + `PRIVACY.pt-BR.md` — matching clarity about non-transmission.

#### Infrastructure (@devops)

- **Network volume upload** (`mqqgzwnfp1` region-locked): ~29 GB total (Qwen UNet fp8 + VAE + Qwen2.5-VL encoder + Lightning LoRA). Additive; does not disturb existing schnell files.
- **RunPod worker image rebuild**: new Docker tag with updated ComfyUI custom nodes if needed for Qwen workflow support. Deploy as `serverless:0.2.0`.
- **No new Cloudflare secrets** (no BFL / no Gemini).
- **No new KV namespaces.**
- **No new RunPod endpoints** — same endpoint, same GPU, same region.

---

## 5. Known Gotchas & Mitigations

Base Qwen-Image-Edit has documented production-quality issues. All are engineering-tractable and accepted per ADR-0003.

| Gotcha | Root cause | Mitigation in this plan |
|---|---|---|
| Aspect ratio / zoom drift between input and output | Qwen latent encoding doesn't perfectly preserve source dimensions | Handler post-processes output: resize to match input dimensions before base64-encoding |
| Background cropping / outpainting on simple edits | Model sometimes "rewrites" unchanged regions | SDK documentation + API reference include prompt-engineering guidance (*"keep background unchanged"*). Future Phase-2: ControlNet canny/depth overlay to structurally pin input |
| 1 megapixel input cap | Model constraint | SDK enforces client-side with auto-downsample; handler also validates defensively |
| Square 1:1 aspect ratio breaks coherence | Specific model failure mode | SDK and handler reject 1:1 with a clear `ValidationError` pointing to supported ratios |
| "Plastic-y feel" on base model | Distillation / base training artifact | Use Qwen-Image-Edit 2509 or 2511 checkpoint (community-reported improvement). Future Phase-3: fine-tune on portfolio-specific data when a specific app justifies the training investment |
| Sequential edit degradation | Model not designed for iterative workflows | Docs recommend single-call comprehensive edits. Future API: optional `final_pass` flag to compose multiple edits server-side in one inference pass |

---

## 6. Economics

### Per-image unit cost on RunPod Serverless RTX 4090 Flex ($0.000261/sec)

| Path | Inference time @ 1024² | Cost / image |
|---|---|---|
| T2I — FLUX.1-schnell fp8, 4 steps | ~3.5s warm | **$0.00091** (unchanged) |
| **i2i — Qwen-Image-Edit fp8 + Lightning 8-step LoRA** | **~34s warm** | **~$0.00887** |
| i2i — Qwen-Image-Edit fp8, 50 steps (no LoRA) | ~71s warm | ~$0.0185 |

### Monthly cost vs rejected hosted-API options at portfolio scale

| Volume / month | Qwen self-host | Gemini 2.5 Std | BFL FLUX.2 [pro] | Annual savings vs Gemini | Annual savings vs BFL |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 10.000 | $88.70 | $395 | $450 | $3.676 | $4.336 |
| 100.000 | $887 | $3.950 | $4.500 | **$36.756** | **$43.356** |
| 1.000.000 | $8.870 | $39.500 | $45.000 | **$367.560** | **$433.560** |

### Fixed costs

- Network volume storage: **~$2/month** additional (29 GB × $0.07/GB)
- Cold-start cost with larger weights: small increase in per-cold-start GPU time; amortized across warm invocations remains negligible at alpha volumes. Monitor at 30-day review.

---

## 7. Phased Implementation Plan

### Phase 0 — ADR + product review (½ dev-day)

- ✅ **ADR-0003 drafted** (`docs/architecture/adr-0003-image-to-image-model-selection.md`) — current status: Proposed
- **@pm Morgan review:** Apache 2.0 license scope across portfolio apps (should be quick — license is unconditional). Validate TERMS/PRIVACY updates do not require legal review beyond existing framework.
- Exit gate: ADR-0003 status advances to Accepted.

### Phase 1 — Story drafting (@sm → @po)

- Draft story: `3.1.qwen-image-edit-i2i.story.md` (or tucked into Epic 3 if scope grows).
- Scope: Phase 2 + Phase 3 below, as a single story. Estimated 4-5 dev-days.
- 10-point validation per `story-lifecycle.md` → `Ready`.

### Phase 2 — Infrastructure prep (@devops, ~1 hour)

- Upload Qwen weights (UNet fp8, VAE, Qwen2.5-VL encoder) + Lightning LoRA to network volume.
- Verify ComfyUI can load them via manual Pod test (`pod.sh up`, then run the workflow once end-to-end).
- Document location in `docs/usage/runpod-serverless-deploy.md`.

### Phase 3 — Implementation (@dev, ~3 dev-days)

Suggested ordering:

1. `serverless/handler.py` — `normalize_input` extension + `build_qwen_edit_workflow()` + dispatch + post-processing.
2. `serverless/tests/` — pytest coverage for new branch, including edge cases (1:1 rejection, >1 MP rejection, missing fields).
3. Integration test: submit a real i2i job against the live RunPod endpoint; verify `image_b64` inline response.
4. SDK `edit()` method + types + vitest.
5. Docs (`docs/api/reference.md`, `sdk/README.md`, `sdk/README.pt-BR.md`, TERMS and PRIVACY small updates).
6. CodeRabbit self-healing loop (max 2 iterations per `coderabbit-integration.md`).

### Phase 4 — QA Gate (@qa, ~½ dev-day)

7-check gate per `story-lifecycle.md`. Focus areas:

- **Regression:** T2I path is byte-identical; schnell latency unchanged; cost per T2I image unchanged.
- **Correctness:** all 6 mapped status values (queued/running/completed/failed/cancelled/timeout) behave deterministically for i2i path.
- **Known gotchas:** aspect-ratio mitigation works (output matches input dims); 1:1 rejection surfaces clean error; >1 MP inputs auto-downsample.
- **Security:** no image bytes in logs; no prompt content in logs.
- **Performance:** inference time within expected envelope (34-70s warm; ≤ 180s cold); rate-limit unchanged.

### Phase 5 — Release (@devops, ~1 hour)

- Redeploy RunPod worker image (`serverless:0.2.0`).
- Publish SDK v0.3.0 to GitHub Packages.
- Tag `v0.3.0-alpha` on the repo.
- Update landing page / API reference on Vercel.
- Post-deploy smoke: real i2i call end-to-end with a test image.

### Phase 6 — 30/90-day review hooks

- Record baseline metrics at release: per-image latency, cold-start frequency, first 100 real i2i jobs' quality signal (user feedback / issue reports).
- 30-day review (aligns with Epic 2 governance, 2026-05-21): re-evaluate cold-start budget impact of the added weights.
- 90-day review (~2026-07-23 per ADR-0003): re-evaluate model-swap candidates (Qwen v2512+, Z-Image-Edit, Step1X-Edit).

**Total estimated effort Phase 0-5: ~4-5 dev-days** across all agents.

---

## 8. Risk Matrix

| # | Risk | Severity | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|
| R1 | Qwen-Image-Edit quality visibly below Flux Kontext on some user prompts | MEDIUM | Transparent documentation of known gotchas. 90-day review re-benchmarks against newer releases (Z-Image-Edit, Step1X-Edit, Qwen successors). Fine-tune as Phase-3 investment for specific high-volume apps. |
| R2 | Cold start time degrades materially with larger weights on volume | MEDIUM | Measure baseline at release + 30-day review. If sustained >180s cold, evaluate Path B warm-always from ADR-0001 or model quantization tightening. |
| R3 | Aspect-ratio drift mitigation (post-process resize) introduces visual artifacts | LOW | Integration test covers common aspect ratios; QA gate validates visually; if artifacts surface, swap to content-aware resize or reject mismatched output with explicit error. |
| R4 | 1 MP input cap is too restrictive for users expecting high-res editing | MEDIUM | Document prominently; future Phase-2 adds tile-and-composite path for high-res. |
| R5 | Lightning 8-step LoRA introduces quality regression vs 50-step baseline | LOW-MEDIUM | Keep 50-step path available as opt-in `high_quality` mode at higher cost. |
| R6 | Qwen2.5-VL Apache 2.0 license questioned legally later | LOW | Apache 2.0 is well-established; Qwen team publicly stated scope. @pm validates in Phase 0 before Phase 1 story drafts. |
| R7 | Lightning LoRA's specific license unclear | LOW-MEDIUM | Phase 2 infrastructure prep includes license verification of the chosen LoRA file; reject any non-Apache/MIT source; if all available LoRAs are problematic, fall back to 50-step path. |
| R8 | User-submitted PII / sensitive images on our infra | LOW | CON-4 already prohibits logging image bytes; no change to data-handling posture. Document that data never leaves our infrastructure — which is strictly better than the BFL-API-route alternative. |
| R9 | Cost at very high scale still includes fixed $2/mo storage + GPU time | LOW | Monitored via 30/90-day review. At 1M imgs/mo, storage is $2/mo out of ~$9k/mo total — negligible. |
| R10 | Team unfamiliar with ComfyUI Qwen workflow specifics | LOW | ComfyUI native workflow + official Qwen deployment docs available; community has documented the setup in multiple independent sources. |

---

| Phase | Agent | Task |

Consultative: **@data-engineer (Dara)** — not required (no DB changes). **@ux-design-expert (Uma)** — only if landing page or demo page gets an i2i section.

---

## 10. Summary — Why This Is the Right Path

1. **License is clean and portfolio-ready.** Apache 2.0 on the model, VAE, text encoder, and LoRA — no MAU caps, no attribution requirements, no TOS revocation risk. Every future app in the portfolio can reuse this without renegotiation.
2. **Economics scale sublinearly.** ~$0.009/image at any volume. No vendor invoice grows with success. Projected savings at portfolio scale (100k imgs/mo across apps): **~$40k/year** vs hosted APIs.
3. **Single runtime, single deploy.** T2I and i2i share the same ComfyUI-on-RunPod environment; operational surface doesn't expand.
4. **Existing contracts preserved.** Async submit/poll (ADR-0002), image_b64 inline (AD-1), SDK retry, rate-limit — all untouched.
5. **Data residency simplifies legal posture.** User images never leave our infrastructure. TERMS/PRIVACY require no third-party data-processor clause for i2i.
6. **Reversible if needed.** Multi-provider gateway shape was introduced during v0.2 (BFL) exploration and retained — if a premium hosted-API tier becomes product-justified later, it adds alongside, not replaces.
7. **Quality gap is documented and engineerable.** ~15-20% below Flux Kontext on worst-case prompts; known gotchas all have concrete mitigations; 90-day review clock validates against newer open-weight releases.
8. **Effort ~4-5 dev-days** — one-time cost, amortized across every future portfolio app that uses this capability.

---
